description_long: This church features a traditional stone construction with a red-tiled gabled roof and a prominent bell tower on one side. The facade includes a large arched doorway, a circular window above it, and subtle stone detailing around the edges. Along the sides, narrow rectangular windows punctuate the otherwise solid stone walls, and a cylindrical turret is attached near the bell tower. The rear of the church displays three semi-cylindrical apses, each with small windows, and a smaller annex with its own tiled roof. The bell tower is topped with a pointed spire and open arches, housing a visible bell. The structure sits on a stone-paved base, suggesting a historic European architectural influence.
description: A stone church with a red-tiled roof, bell tower, arched entrance, and semi-cylindrical rear apses, set on a stone base. Features include narrow windows, a circular window, and a prominent spire.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main arched entrance, circular window above, and the bell tower with spire on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the bell tower, cylindrical turret, and narrow rectangular windows along the length of the building.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals three semi-cylindrical apses and the smaller annex with its own roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the annex, more narrow windows, and the bell tower in the background.
